claim: Claude Opus 4.6 sustains agentic tasks for longer than previous versions, enabling more complex multi-step reasoning
actionable: Use Opus 4.6 for complex agentic tasks that require sustained reasoning across many steps

claim: Opus 4.6 has 1M token context window (beta) and maintains quality across million tokens better than previous models, with less context rot
actionable: Use Opus 4.6 for large codebases and document-heavy tasks that need 500K+ context; it maintains coherence better than alternatives

claim: Agent teams allow multiple Claude instances to work independently and communicate directly with each other, unlike sub-agents that report back to main agent
actionable: Use agent teams for parallel exploration tasks (research different hypotheses, debug multiple approaches) where inter-team communication adds value

claim: Agent teams use significantly more tokens than single sessions because each agent runs in its own context window
actionable: Use agent teams only when parallel exploration genuinely adds value (research, debugging competing hypotheses); use sub-agents for simpler task delegation

claim: Opus 4.6 pricing same as 4.5: $5/$10 per million input tokens (under/over 200K threshold), $25/$37.50 per million output tokens
actionable: Budget for Opus 4.6 at the same rates as 4.5; use prompt caching to reduce costs on large context windows

claim: Opus 4.6 performs much better at retrieving relevant info from large document sets with less drift than 4.5 (93% accuracy at 256K tokens, 76% at 1M)
actionable: Use Opus 4.6 for RAG over large document collections; it reliably retrieves needle-in-haystack information across million-token contexts

claim: Opus 4.6 introduces adaptive thinking that adjusts model thinking amount up and down based on task complexity during inference
actionable: Use the slash-effort control with Opus 4.6 to balance intelligence, speed, and cost based on task requirements

claim: Claude is now integrated into Excel and PowerPoint (via Claude in Office), not just specialized tools, making it applicable to everyday workflows
actionable: Train users on using Claude directly inside Excel and PowerPoint for document work rather than exporting to Claude chat
